scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE PRINCIPAL JUDGE, CITY CIVIL COURT, BENGALURU, KARNATAKA

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} OF 2024

BETWEEN:

Ram Kumar,
S/o {{FATHER_NAME}},
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{OCCUPATION}},
Residing at Jayanagar, Bengaluru – {{PIN_CODE}}.

... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
S/o {{FATHER_NAME}},
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{OCCUPATION}},
Residing at Koramangala, Bengaluru – {{PIN_CODE}}.

... DEFENDANT

PLAINT UNDER ORDER VII OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908 FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY (HAND LOAN)

The Plaintiff respectfully states as follows:

1. The Plaintiff is a resident of Bengaluru.

2. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru.

3. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs.15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant as a hand loan.

4. The said amount was transferred by the Plaintiff to the Defendant through NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456 on 15.03.2024.

5. There was no written agreement between the parties. The loan was advanced on the Defendant’s request and on the Defendant’s assurance of repayment.

6. The Defendant acknowledged receipt of the said hand loan through WhatsApp messages.

7. It was orally understood/assured that the Defendant would repay the hand loan on or before 20.06.2024. However, the Defendant failed to repay the amount by 20.06.2024 and has remained in default thereafter.

8. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands calling upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding amount. As the Defendant did not repay, the Plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be issued to the Defendant demanding repayment.

9. Despite the said notice and repeated demands, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount. The Defendant is liable to pay the principal sum of Rs.15,00,000/-.

10. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal sum from 20.06.2024 (date of default) till the date of filing of the suit, and further prays for pendente lite and future interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit.

CAUSE OF ACTION

11. The cause of action for the suit arose on 15.03.2024 when the Plaintiff advanced the hand loan to the Defendant. The cause of action further arose on 20.06.2024 when the amount became due and the Defendant failed to repay, and again on 01.07.2024 when the Plaintiff issued legal notice. The cause of action continues as the Defendant has not repaid the amount.

LIMITATION

12. The suit is filed within the period of limitation, being within three years from 20.06.2024 when the amount became due and the Defendant committed default.

JURISDICTION

13. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides within Bengaluru and the transaction and default occurred within Bengaluru. This Hon’ble Court has jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

14. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit is valued at Rs.15,00,000/-.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

15. For the purpose of court fee and pecuniary jurisdiction, the suit is valued at Rs.15,00,000/-. Court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid herewith as per the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

PRAYER

Wherefore, the Plaintiff prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a judgment and decree directing the Defendant to pay the Plaintiff a sum of Rs.15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only);

(b) Award pre-suit interest at 12% per annum on Rs.15,00,000/- from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing of the suit;

(c) Award pendente lite interest from the date of filing of the suit till the date of decree, and future interest from the date of decree till realization, at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit;

(d) Award costs of the suit; and

(e) Grant such other reliefs as this Hon’ble Court deems fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

The Plaintiff relies upon the following documents:

1. Bank transaction record showing NEFT transfer with UTR No. AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.2024.
2. Printouts of WhatsApp messages acknowledging receipt of the hand loan.
3. Copy of legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VERIFICATION

I, Ram Kumar, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 10 are true to my personal knowledge and belief, and paragraphs 11 to 15 are believed to be true on legal advice and information.

Verified at Bengaluru on {{DATE_OF_VERIFY}}.

PLACE: BENGALURU
DATE: {{DATE_OF_VERIFY}}

________________________
Plaintiff

ADVOCATE FOR PLAINTIFF

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
{{ENROLLMENT_NO}}
{{CHAMBER_ADDRESS}}
